claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'd like to help you determine if you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

To start, **how old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask: **Is Alex in any approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but at 21, the rules would be different.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in education or training. He's actually working full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review the situation:
- Alex is 21 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He is working full-time

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit. Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 (under 20) if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21, he is beyond the maximum age eligibility, even if he were in education or training.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, eligibility can continue only if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his education or training status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. He is not in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, which requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, eligibility can continue only if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his education or training status.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. He is not in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, which requires children to be either under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
